sections.paragraph:
Exo 28:43 34:7 more rarely עָוֹון 2Ki 7:9 Psa 51:7 const. עֲֹון, עֲוֹון 1Ch 21:8, pl. absol. and const. עֲֹונֹות with suff. עֲֹונֵינוּ, עֲֹונֶיךָ, more often עֲֹונֹתַי, עֲֹונֹתֶיךָ etc.; m. pr. perversity, depravity (from the root עָוָה); hence-

(1) a depraved action, a crime, a sin, Gen 4:13 44:16 . Job 31:11, עֲֹון פְּלִילִים “a crime to be punished by the judges,” comp. Job 31:28 19:29 , עֲֹונֹות חֶרֶב “crimes to be punished by the sword.” Eze 21:30, עֲֹון קֵץ “crime of end,” i.e. which brings an end or destruction. Eze. 21:34 35:5 . It is often guilt contracted by sinning, as עֲֹון אָבֹות “the guilt of the fathers,” Exo 20:5 34:7 עֲֹון הָאֱמֹרִי “the guilt of the Amorites,” Gen 15:16. עֲֹון חַמָּאתִי “the guilt of my sin,” Psa 32:5 also any thing unjustly acquired, Hos 12:9, “they shall not find in my possession עָֹון אֲשֶׁר חֵטְא any thing unjustly acquired which (would be) sin,” (kein Unrecht, das Sünde wäre). In speaking of pardon and expiation of sin, the words סָלַח, הֶעֱבִיר, כִּפֵּר, נָשָׂא No. 2, c, are used; of punishing it, the verb פָּקַד is used; of bearing or suffering its penalty, the verb נָשָׂא No. 2, b.

(2) Sometimes it is the penalty of sin, Isa 5:18 calamity, misery, Psa 31:11. [The common meaning does very well in this place.]
